[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Gotemba%2C+Shizuoka%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Gotemba%2C+Shizuoka%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Gotemba%2C+Shizuoka%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Gotemba%2C+Shizuoka%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(June 2012)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(御殿場市, *Gotenba-shi*; sometimes **Gotenba**) is a [city](/wiki/Cities_of_Japan "Cities of Japan") on the southeastern flank of [Mount Fuji](/wiki/Mount_Fuji "Mount Fuji") in [Shizuoka Prefecture](/wiki/Shizuoka_Prefecture "Shizuoka Prefecture"), [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"). As of 30 June 2019[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit), the city had an estimated [population](/wiki/Population "Population") of 88,370 in 36,096 households,[[1]](#cite_note-1) and a [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") of 450 persons per km². The total area of the city is 194.90 square kilometres (75.25 sq mi).

**Gotemba** (御殿場市, *Gotenba-shi*; sometimes **Gotenba**) is a [city](/wiki/Cities_of_Japan "Cities of Japan") on the southeastern flank of [Mount Fuji](/wiki/Mount_Fuji "Mount Fuji") in [Shizuoka Prefecture](/wiki/Shizuoka_Prefecture "Shizuoka Prefecture"), [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan"). As of 30 June 2019[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit), the city had an estimated [population](/wiki/Population "Population") of 88,370 in 36,096 households,[[1]](#cite_note-1) and a [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") of 450 persons per km². The total area of the city is 194.90 square kilometres (75.25 sq mi).

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

Gotemba is located in far eastern Shizuoka Prefecture at an altitude of 250–600 metres (820–1,970 ft). [Mount Fuji](/wiki/Mount_Fuji "Mount Fuji") is located to the west, and [Mount Hakone](/wiki/Mount_Hakone "Mount Hakone") to the east, with the [Tanzawa Mountains](/wiki/Tanzawa_Mountains "Tanzawa Mountains") to the north and [Mount Ashitaka](/wiki/Mount_Ashitaka "Mount Ashitaka") to the south. The area is noted for its numerous [golf courses](/wiki/Golf_course "Golf course"), with the [Mitsui Sumitomo Visa Taiheiyo Masters](/wiki/Mitsui_Sumitomo_Visa_Taiheiyo_Masters "Mitsui Sumitomo Visa Taiheiyo Masters") taking place annually.

### Surrounding municipalities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Surrounding municipalities")]

* [Kanagawa Prefecture](/wiki/Kanagawa_Prefecture "Kanagawa Prefecture")
  + [Hakone](/wiki/Hakone,_Kanagawa "Hakone, Kanagawa")
* Shizuoka Prefecture
  + [Fuji](/wiki/Fuji,_Shizuoka "Fuji, Shizuoka")
  + [Fujinomiya](/wiki/Fujinomiya,_Shizuoka "Fujinomiya, Shizuoka")
  + [Oyama](/wiki/Oyama,_Shizuoka "Oyama, Shizuoka")
  + [Susono](/wiki/Susono,_Shizuoka "Susono, Shizuoka")

### Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Demographics")]

Per Japanese census data,[[2]](#cite_note-2) the population of Gotemba has grown over the past 50 years.

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Climate")]

The city has a climate characterized by hot and humid summers, and relatively mild winters ([Köppen climate classification](/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification "Köppen climate classification") *Cfa*). The average annual temperature in Gotemba is 13.2 °C (55.8 °F). The average annual rainfall is 2,874.6 mm (113.17 in) with September as the wettest month. The temperatures are highest on average in August, at around 24.1 °C (75.4 °F), and lowest in January, at around 2.7 °C (36.9 °F).[[3]](#cite_note-normals-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2e/Gotemba_downtown_before_1945.JPG/250px-Gotemba_downtown_before_1945.JPG)](/wiki/File:Gotemba_downtown_before_1945.JPG)

Gotemba in the early 1940s

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/ce/A_street_in_Gotemba.jpg/250px-A_street_in_Gotemba.jpg)](/wiki/File:A_street_in_Gotemba.jpg)

A street in Gotemba

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/89/Gotemba-city-office.JPG/250px-Gotemba-city-office.JPG)](/wiki/File:Gotemba-city-office.JPG)

Gotemba City Hall

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c4/View_of_gotemba.jpg/250px-View_of_gotemba.jpg)](/wiki/File:View_of_gotemba.jpg)

[Mount Fuji](/wiki/Mount_Fuji "Mount Fuji") and city of Gotemba

Gotemba was the location of the earliest known settlement founded in the region of Shizuoka which dates back 2,000 years ago. The inhabitants of the area (including Gotemba) were not ethnically [Yamato](/wiki/Yamato_people "Yamato people") and were likely [Ainu](/wiki/Ainu_people "Ainu people").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Gotemba was the site of a *[shōen](/wiki/Sh%C5%8Den "Shōen")* belonging to [Ise Shrine](/wiki/Ise_Shrine "Ise Shrine") in the [Heian period](/wiki/Heian_period "Heian period"). During the [Edo period](/wiki/Edo_period "Edo period") the area was part of [Odawara Domain](/wiki/Odawara_Domain "Odawara Domain"). After the start of the [Meiji period](/wiki/Meiji_period "Meiji period"), it became part of [Shizuoka Domain](/wiki/Shizuoka_Domain "Shizuoka Domain") until the [abolition of the han system](/wiki/Abolition_of_the_han_system "Abolition of the han system") in 1871 and the establishment of Shizuoka Prefecture. The opening of [Gotemba Station](/wiki/Gotemba_Station "Gotemba Station") on what was then the Tōkaidō Main Line on February 1, 1889, spurred development of the area. With the establishment of the modern municipalities system on April 1, 1889, Gotemba Town was created within [Suntō District](/wiki/Sunt%C5%8D_District,_Shizuoka "Suntō District, Shizuoka"), along with Fujioka Village, Harasato Village, Ino Village, Tamaho Village, and Takane Village.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

However, the opening of the [Tanna Tunnel](/wiki/Tanna_Tunnel "Tanna Tunnel") on December 1, 1934, resulted in the route of Tōkaidō Main Line shifting south to [Atami](/wiki/Atami,_Shizuoka "Atami, Shizuoka"), leaving Gotemba on the spur [Gotemba Line](/wiki/Gotemba_Line "Gotemba Line"), which resulted in temporary depopulation. During the pre-war era, Gotemba was a major base area for the [Imperial Japanese Army](/wiki/Imperial_Japanese_Army "Imperial Japanese Army"), and still houses military facilities and a large training area (the [East Fuji Maneuver Area](/wiki/East_Fuji_Maneuver_Area "East Fuji Maneuver Area")) for the modern [Japanese Ground Self-Defense Force](/wiki/Japanese_Ground_Self-Defense_Force "Japanese Ground Self-Defense Force") and the [United States Marine Corps](/wiki/United_States_Marine_Corps "United States Marine Corps").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Gotemba was elevated to city status on February 11, 1955, through the merger of Gotemba Town with neighboring Fujioka Village, Harasato Village, Ino Village, and Tamaho Village. The city expanded through annexation of Takane Village on January 1, 1956 and the Furusawa District of [Oyama Town](/wiki/Oyama,_Shizuoka "Oyama, Shizuoka") on September 1, 1957. The Gotemba Interchange on the Tōmei Expressway was completed on May 25, 1969.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Government")]

Gotemba has a [mayor-council](/wiki/Mayor-council "Mayor-council") form of government with a directly elected mayor and a [unicameral](/wiki/Unicameral "Unicameral") city legislature of 21 members.

## Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Economy")]

Gotemba has a mixed economy. Agriculture has traditionally been dominated by [rice](/wiki/Rice "Rice"), irrigated by water from Mount Fuji, and has been supplemented by the growing of various green vegetables and the raising of pigs. Development of light industries, especially in food processing and electronics has profited through Gotemba's location on the Tōmei Expressway, and Gotemba is noted for a number of golf courses and a spacious outlet shopping center.

Retailing company Chelsea Japan rented former Gotemba Family Land Amusement Park, and officially opened in July 2000 as [Gotemba Premium Outlets](/wiki/Gotemba_Premium_Outlets "Gotemba Premium Outlets").[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gotemba,_Shizuoka&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Education")]

Gotemba has ten public elementary schools and six public junior high schools operated by the city government, and two public high schools operated by the Shizuoka Prefectural Board of Education. The city also has one private high school, and the prefecture also operates one special education school for the disabled.
